name: web-frontend-testing
description: Orchestrated Playwright workflow for web frontend testing: guided requirements intake, codebase surface scan, standardized test plan, default-agent fallback gates through MCP, Playwright CLI execution (preferred) with optional visible session and pre-test manual authentication, Playwright MCP exploration, dual engineering/executive reporting, and an interactive MCP report viewer.

interface.shortDescription: Plan, run, and report Playwright CLI (preferred) or MCP tests for web frontends.
interface.longDescription: User-facing orchestrator that walks a five-stage Playwright workflow (intake → codebase scan → plan → execute → report). It auto-detects testable surfaces from the repository, generates a standardized test-plan.yaml, prefers Playwright CLI execution (existing specs, generated specs from the plan, plan commands, or grep targets) with optional visible CLI sessions and an optional pre-test manual authentication session so the user can sign in before tests run, falls back to Playwright MCP for live exploration or MCP evidence capture, emits both an engineering Markdown report and a self-contained accessible executive HTML summary, and can open those outputs in the bundled interactive MCP report viewer. When a host cannot select the custom orchestrator agent, default agents should start with the web-frontend-testing-workflow MCP server, which enforces intake validation, plan hash approvals, destructive/production gates, and one-target-at-a-time execution before the host runs CLI or Playwright MCP actions.
defaultPrompt: Scan this repository and propose a Playwright CLI test plan for the frontend.
Run the approved CLI scenarios against http://localhost:3000 and capture artifacts.
Start a visible Playwright CLI session so I can sign in manually before tests run.
Convert approved scenarios into Playwright CLI specs under tests/web-frontend.
Explore the frontend interactively with Playwright MCP and capture evidence.
Generate the engineering and executive reports for the latest test run.
Open the interactive report viewer for the latest web frontend test report.
If the web-frontend-testing custom agent is unavailable, use web-frontend-testing-workflow/start_workflow and follow the default-agent fallback runbook.
